title: 'make validate': require PRs to include tests
body:
This is an almost-clone of podman PR 8911[1], in which Ed
ticks off developers by requiring that PRs include tests.

 [1] https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/8911

Motivated by the just-merged #2973, which is exactly the sort
of tiny little change that can have huge consequences down
the line and/or be easy to undo.

Changes from the podman version: allow '[NO NEW TESTS NEEDED]'
in addition to just '[NO TESTS NEEDED]', and make the former
the default suggestion. It's clearer. And, of course, update
the test-suite cases to reflect the buildah git tree.

Signed-off-by: Ed Santiago <santiago@redhat.com>

comments.body:
No actual way to tell if the test was invoked, because it's an `@` rule, but at least it [didn't fail](https://github.com/containers/buildah/pull/2985).

I humbly suggest that `@` is not really desirable in this target: not only is it impossible to know what has run and passed, in the case where it runs and fails, it is impossible to know the precise incantation of what the failed command was (esp. if it has arguments, which right now nothing does). May I remove them and re-push?

Thanks @rhatdan, I've removed the '@'s.

@TomSweeneyRedHat rebase not actually needed, 'out of date' != 'you have conflicts', but I rebased anyway.
The ubuntu-2010 test failed, looked like a flake, and has been restarted.

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** Approval requirements bypassed by manually added approval. This pull-request has been approved by: *edsantiago*, *rhatdan* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fbuildah). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - **[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/buildah/blob/master/OWNERS)** Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
